title: lessingia germanorum subsp. eulemmonii
intro: Lessingia germanorum subsp. eulemmonii, also known as Western or California fleabane, is a flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It is native to the western coast of North America and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and as an anti-inflammatory agent. The plant's leaves, flowers, and roots contain compounds with antimicrobial and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and skin irritations. Traditionally, Western or California fleabane has been prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal benefits, and it is also used in herbal remedies for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
